name: Boeuf Bourguignonne
recipeIngredient: 3 pounds beef round, cut into 2-inch cubes
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine wine, 2 tablespoons olive oil, salt, pepper, thyme, and bay leaf in a large bowl.
2. Slice one onion, one carrot, one clove of garlic, and celery. Add the vegetables to the wine mixture.
3. Marinate beef in this mixture for at least two hours up to 24 hours, turning occasionally.
4. Remove meat from the marinade and pat it dry. Strain the marinade and reserve it.
5. Heat 2 tablespoons butter with olive oil in a heavy skillet over medium heat. Brown the meat quickly on all sides. Transfer the meat to a two-quart baking dish.
6. Deglaze the skillet with the reserved marinade and add it to the baking dish.
7. Chop the remaining onions, carrot, and garlic finely. Melt 1/4 cup butter in the skillet and sauté the garlic, onions, and carrots until lightly browned (about 5 minutes).
8. Blend in flour and stir for one minute.
9. Add the marinade, beef broth, and tomato paste to the skillet. Stir until the mixture comes to a boil.
10. Pour the sauce over the meat. Cover with foil and bake at 350°F for 2-1/2 hours.
11. Melt 1 tablespoon butter in a separate skillet over medium heat. Sauté the white onions with salt and sugar until golden. Add mushrooms and sauté for two minutes more.
12. Add the sautéed white onions, mushrooms, and tomatoes to the beef. Continue baking for an additional 10 minutes.
